subject: Urgent: Need to downgrade from Pro (18 seats) to Lite (6 seats) WITHOUT losing email history — Workspace: LoopStack
email:
Hi Hiver Support,

I'm reaching out on behalf of LoopStack (workspace ID: loopstack-hiver-prod), and honestly I'm pretty frustrated because I've been going in circles trying to figure this out on my own for the past two days. We're a seed-stage startup and our runway just got a lot shorter, so we need to cut our Hiver subscription from 18 Pro seats down to 6 seats immediately — ideally before our next billing cycle on June 14th.

My biggest concern is our shared mailbox history at support@loopstack.io. We have over 14 months of customer conversations, closed tickets, and tagged threads in there that our team absolutely cannot lose — this is critical institutional knowledge for us. I've read conflicting things on your help docs about whether email history is preserved on Lite or if it gets archived/locked after downgrading, and I need a definitive answer before I do anything. On top of that, I need to know exactly which 6 agent seats to keep and how to remove the other 12 without accidentally deleting conversations those agents were assigned to. We're currently on the Pro plan (ticket ref: #THD-20814) and I do NOT want any automations, SLA rules, or tags to get wiped during this process either. Please don't send me a generic help article — I need someone to walk me through this step by step because the stakes are too high to get wrong.
expected_response:
Hi Priya,

Thank you for writing in, and I'm sorry to hear you've been stressed trying to navigate this — I completely understand how high the stakes feel when both budget pressure and critical data are on the line. I want to give you clear, definitive answers so you can move forward with confidence.

**Ticket reference: #THD-20814 | Workspace: loopstack-hiver-prod**

Here's everything you need to know, step by step:

---

**1. Will your email history be preserved after downgrading to Lite?**
Yes — definitively. Downgrading your plan does NOT delete or lock your existing email history in support@loopstack.io. All past conversations, closed threads, tags, and notes remain fully visible and searchable for all active agents on the workspace, regardless of plan tier. The Lite plan does impose limits on certain *features going forward* (e.g., advanced analytics, SLA policies, some automation rules), but your 14 months of historical data will remain intact and accessible.

**2. What happens to conversations assigned to removed agents?**
This is the critical step to handle carefully. Before you remove any of the 12 agents, please do the following:
- Go to **Settings → Members** in your Hiver dashboard.
- For each agent you plan to remove, first **reassign their open conversations** to one of the 6 remaining agents. You can do this in bulk using the conversation filter: filter by Assignee → select the agent → select all → bulk reassign.
- Once all open/pending conversations are reassigned, you can safely remove the agent. Closed/resolved conversations they were previously assigned to will remain in the mailbox history and will NOT be deleted.

**3. What happens to your automations, SLA rules, and tags?**
Here's where you do need to take action before downgrading:
- **Tags:** Tags are workspace-level and will be preserved regardless of plan.
- **Automations:** The Lite plan supports up to 5 automations. If you currently have more than 5 active automations, the extras will be *disabled* (not deleted) after downgrade. I'd recommend auditing your automations now at **Settings → Automations** and deciding which 5 to keep active. Disabled automations can be re-enabled if you upgrade again.
- **SLA Policies:** SLA management is a Pro/Enterprise feature. Your existing SLA configurations will be saved in your account settings but will become inactive on Lite. They won't be deleted, so they'd be restored upon any future upgrade.

**4. How to process the downgrade before June 14th:**
- Complete the agent reassignment steps above first.
- Then go to **Settings → Billing → Manage Subscription** and select **Change Plan**.
- Choose Lite and adjust your seat count to 6.
- The change will take effect at the start of your next billing cycle (June 14th), and you'll receive a prorated credit for the 12 removed seats for any remaining days in the current cycle.

If you'd prefer, I can also arrange for one of our billing specialists to jump on a 20-minute screen-share call to walk you through this live — just let me know and I'll schedule it within the next 24 hours.

For your reference, here are the relevant help docs:
- [Managing seats and removing agents](#)
- [Hiver plan comparison and feature limits](#)
- [Bulk reassigning conversations](#)

Priya, I know this is a stressful moment for LoopStack, and I want to make sure this transition goes smoothly. Please reply here or reach out directly if anything is unclear — I'll personally monitor this thread (#THD-20814) through your billing date.

Warm regards,
**Alex Mercer**
Senior Support Specialist, Hiver
support@hiverhq.com | Response SLA: 4 hours (Business Hours)

expected_response:
Hi Priya,

Thank you for reaching out and for putting together such a detailed breakdown — this makes it much easier to investigate accurately. I've pulled up Invoice #INV-2024-08841 for workspace 'stripe-ops-support' and I'll walk through each of your questions below.

**1. Proration charge ($47.20 — 2 seats, Aug 14–31)**
Your Pro plan is billed at $29.00/seat/month. For the period Aug 14–31, that's 18 days out of a 31-day billing cycle. The proration for 2 seats works out to: 2 × $29.00 × (18/31) = **$33.68**. The $47.20 figure does not match this calculation, which suggests there may have been an error in how the seat addition was recorded — possibly 3 seats were registered instead of 2, or an incorrect daily rate was applied. I'm escalating this to our billing team for a manual audit and will confirm the correct amount within 1 business day.

**2. Sales Tax — California vs. Delaware**
This is a great catch. Tax nexus is determined by the billing address on file at the time of invoice generation. It's possible your billing profile still has a California address associated with a previous payment method or contact record. I'd recommend navigating to **Settings → Billing → Billing Address** in your Hiver workspace to verify the address currently on file. If it shows a CA address, updating it to your Delaware address and contacting us will allow us to issue a corrected invoice. If it already shows Delaware, this would be a billing system error and we'll issue a credit note immediately.

**3. Invoice total discrepancy ($17.00)**
You're right that $522.00 + $47.20 + $38.64 = $607.84, not $624.84. The $17.00 difference is unexplained in the current invoice data I can see, and I want to make sure we don't close this until it's fully accounted for. This will be included in the billing audit I'm initiating for point #1.

**Next steps:**
- I've flagged thread THD-558821 as a billing audit case — our billing specialist will review all three discrepancies together.
- If you have a screenshot of the invoice, please do reply with it attached; it will help the team cross-reference faster.
- You can expect a follow-up from us within **1 business day** with either a corrected invoice or a detailed explanation for each line item.

I understand this needs to be clean before it goes to your finance team, and we'll make sure it is. Please don't hesitate to reply here if anything else comes up in the meantime.
